title: Create POST /api/auth/login and /api/auth/register route handlers
description: Create Next.js App Router handler files at src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts and src/app/api/auth/register/route.ts that expose the existing auth utility functions as HTTP endpoints. The login endpoint must use AuthService (Payload-based PBKDF2), not UserStore. Both handlers must: accept JSON body with { email, password } and { email, password, confirmPassword } respectively, apply SlidingWindowRateLimiter for brute-force protection, return 400 for validation errors, 401 for invalid credentials, 409 for email conflicts, and return { accessToken, refreshToken, user }. Integration test files should be created alongside each route.
existing_patterns: src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts: SlidingWindowRateLimiter + createRateLimiterMiddleware pattern for brute-force protection on auth endpoints
src/auth/auth-service.ts: login() uses Payload.find + PBKDF2 verify + jwtService.signAccessToken/signRefreshToken; returns AuthResult with { accessToken, refreshToken, user }

src/api/auth/register.ts: createError helper returns Error & { status: number }; register() validates email format, password strength, email uniqueness via Payload.find, then calls authService.login()
src/middleware/validation.ts: createValidationMiddleware() helper attaches validated body to NextRequest; used for structured validation
